mnemonics: FLIP-FLOPS: Fast Latches In Pairs - For Lasting Output & State
keyPoints: Latches are level-sensitive and transparent during the active level, while flip-flops are edge-triggered and change state only on a clock edge.
The fundamental building blocks are the SR Latch, D Latch, and the D Flip-Flop, which provides edge-triggered behavior for stable data storage.
Flip-flops are used to build registers and counters, which are the essential memory elements for sequential logic circuits.
commonMistakes: Confusing latches (level-sensitive) with flip-flops (edge-triggered), leading to timing errors and unstable circuit behavior.
Assuming the clock signal is just another input; forgetting its critical role in synchronizing state changes in flip-flops.
